1 / 7

Happy Birthday

A little verse for your day

You do not arrive with trumpet or flame, yet rooms grow softer all the same.

That sort of grace is hard to fake; it warms the air in your quiet wake.

First Light

The welcome came at once

Before I knew the house or door, I knew the feeling at its core.

No fuss, no test, no guarded art, just open faces and an open heart.

Quiet Things

What needs no speech

Some loves are loud for all to see; the truest ones move quietly.

They lean in close, they hold, they stay, and say enough without display.

By The Fire

The kind that feeds the room

Some men make speeches, polished, grand; some feed the room with a steady hand.

The wiser school, to my mind still, is kindness practiced beside a grill.

Later On

The room gave in

Each proper evening, if it goes right, lets dignity loosen up by night.

And once good music makes its case, joy drops the pose and finds its pace.

In Motion

Then music made its case

A photograph can hint, suggest, imply; a moving frame tells you why.

Once music starts and feet agree, the proof is plain for all to see.

For Your Birthday

Happy Birthday

So here is the wish these small scenes bring: more long tables, more laughter, more reasons to sing.

More love in the house, more peace on the way, and joy that keeps finding you day after day.

Tap or click to continue
